Now we need to output JSON with files array. Each file object has word, filename, lines. Filenames are lowercase, spaces to underscores. All words are single words, no spaces. So filenames: noon.md, normal.md, normalization.md, normalize.md, normalized.md, normalizing.md, north.md, nose.md, note.md, notebook.md.

Make sure lines are exactly 4 non-empty strings. Use correct articles in German: "Ein normaler Tag" (masculine), "Eine Nase" (feminine), "Ein Notizbuch" (neuter), "Eine Notiz" (feminine), "Ein normalisierter Wert" (masculine), "Mittag" is masculine? "Mittag ist eine Zeit" - "Mittag" is masculine, but "Zeit" is feminine, so "Mittag ist eine Zeit" is fine. "Norden ist eine Richtung" - "Norden" masculine, "Richtung" feminine. "Normalisierung ist ein Prozess" - "Normalisierung" feminine, "Prozess" masculine, so "ein Prozess". "Normalisieren ist eine Anpassung" - "Normalisieren" neuter? Actually "das Normalisieren" is neuter, but we use "Normalisieren" as subject, then "ist eine Anpassung" - "Anpassung" feminine, so "eine". "Normalisieren ist eine Veränderung" - same. "Eine Notiz ist eine Aufzeichnung" - both feminine. "Ein Notizbuch ist ein Buch" - both neuter. "Ein normaler Tag ist ein Tag" - both masculine. "A normal day" - English uses "A" because day is countable. "A nose" - correct. "A note" - correct. "A notebook" - correct. "North" - no article because uncountable. "Noon" - no article. "Normalization" - no article. "Normalizing" - no article. "Normalized" - adjective phrase "A normalized value" - article.

Japanese: Use appropriate particles. For "正午は時間だ" - "は" topic marker. "普通の日は日だ" - "の" possessive. "正規化はプロセスだ" - "は". "正規化することは調整だ" - "することは" for gerund. "正規化された値は数だ" - "された" past passive attributive. "正規化することは変化だ" - same. "北は方向だ" - "は". "鼻は体の一部だ" - "の一部". "メモは記録だ" - "は". "ノートは本だ" - "は". All fine.

Chinese: Use appropriate measure words? For "正午是时间" - no measure word needed. "正常的一天是一天" - "的" possessive, "是" verb. "归一化是一个过程" - "一个" measure. "归一化是一种调整" - "一种". "归一化的值是一个数字" - "的" possessive, "一个". "归一化是一种变化" - "一种". "北是一个方向" - "一个". "鼻子是身体的一部分" - "的" and "一部分". "笔记是记录" - no measure. "笔记本是书" - no measure? Could say "笔记本是一本书" but "笔记本是书" is fine. To be consistent, we can add "一本" but not necessary. Let's keep as is.
